hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) will enhance SSTR2 selectivity by forming specific π-π stacking or cation-π interactions with ECL2 residues (e.g., Y193), while mutating position 11 to hydrophobic residues (L/I/V) will improve TM5 complementarity and reduce off-target binding.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current design strategy is generating candidates with strong ddG but insufficient SSTR2 selectivity. The next iteration should prioritize disrupting off-target interactions at conserved positions while preserving the pharmacophore and Cys3/Cys14.

proposed_changes.rationale: To improve SSTR2 selectivity, we need to disrupt conserved pocket contacts in SSTR3/SSTR5. These positions are non-pharmacophore and can be mutated to reduce off-target binding while preserving the pharmacophore (positions 7-10 and Cys3/Cys14).

These mutations are designed to break hydrogen bonds and hydrophobic interactions in SSTR3/SSTR5 pockets without affecting the pharmacophore. For example, introducing charged residues at positions 1 and 2 can disrupt conserved salt bridges in off-targets.
